steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses separated by the blank: the first establishes Rita Moreno's achievement (ending with “...the Emmy, Grammy, Oscar, and Tony awards, respectively”), and the second describes what this achievement is called (“...the achievement is known as 'winning the EGOT'”).

steps.title: Apply the joining rule
steps.content:
A semicolon joins two independent clauses. Choice D uses “awards, respectively;” to correctly apply this punctuation.

The other options violate the rule:
  • A uses a comma without a coordinating conjunction (comma splice)
  • C separates the clauses with a dash instead
  • B has a malformed semicolon without proper spacing
.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
